reason:
rust-analyzer scip emits thousands of stderr warning lines. The
original code piped stderr but never read it until after wait_timeout,
causing the OS pipe buffer (~64KB on Windows) to fill and the child
to block on write() — a false timeout no matter how high the limit.

Fix: spawn background threads to drain stdout/stderr continuously while
the main thread waits for exit. Capture the last 8KB of stderr for
error reporting.
summary: fix: drain SCIP process pipes to prevent deadlock

reason:
Decouple test-slow/test-compile-fail from test job (track 0066)

Change needs: [web-build, test] -> needs: [web-build, clippy] for both
test-slow and test-compile-fail jobs (ci.yml:114/146). These are
independent test tiers (--profile slow = __slow tests; --profile
compile-fail = trybuild) that don't need the fast test tier to have
passed. clippy (which compiles --all-targets --all-features) still
fail-fasts a broken build cheaply (~2-6m) before the heavy tiers spend
runner time. web-build stays in needs (real artifact dependency).

This overlaps the two slowest CI jobs with the test matrix instead of
serializing them after it. Measured: ~29m -> ~21.5m PR wall-clock
(~7.5m saved, ~26%). Complements 0062's within-job -j 4 fix (the
between-job lever).

DoD-0: baseline ~29m (heavy tiers start at ~23m after test).
DoD-1: PR #13 CI run 29536848340 all green, heavy tiers start at ~6m
      (after clippy), overlapping test.
DoD-2: ~21.5m total, no concurrency queueing (peak 8 test-family jobs).
DoD-3: PR #14 (throwaway, deleted) compile error -> clippy failed ->
      test-slow + test-compile-fail skipped (fail-fast preserved).
DoD-4: conductor.md/sequencing.md/coordination.md/docs/ToDo.md updated;
      ledger tx committed (INFRA).

Workflow-only diff; no engine/test/crypto.rs changes.

Refs: 0062 (within-job -j fix, done), 0063 (node24 upgrade, done).
